description_long: This cowshed is a rectangular, single-story structure with a gable roof covered in dark gray shingles. The exterior walls are constructed from horizontal wooden planks, giving it a rustic and natural appearance. One side of the building features a large, textured stone or concrete wall, likely serving as a windbreak or additional enclosure. The front of the cowshed is open, supported by sturdy wooden posts, allowing easy access for livestock and ventilation. The roof overhangs slightly, providing some shelter at the entrance. There are no visible windows or elaborate decorative elements, emphasizing its utilitarian function.
description: A rectangular wooden cowshed with a dark gable roof, open front, and a large stone wall on one side.
description_view_0: The front view shows the open entrance supported by wooden posts and the gable roof, with the stone wall visible on the left.
description_view_1: The side view displays the full length of the wooden plank wall and the shingled roof, with no openings or windows.
description_view_2: The rear view reveals a solid wooden wall beneath the gable roof, with no doors or windows visible.
description_view_3: The opposite side view highlights the large stone or concrete wall running the length of the structure, contrasting with the wooden wall on the other side.
